prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

ETH resolving above 2200 on the specified April 17 Binance noon-ET 1-minute close remains the clear base case, but the swarm’s mild below-market view survives synthesis: current Binance price context strongly supports Yes, yet a 94.5% market price still looks a bit too aggressive for a venue-specific single-minute crypto close with sub-24h tail risk still live.

## Why this may matter now

Market implied probability is 0.945. My post-synthesis range is 0.89 to 0.92 Yes. That leaves only a modest below-market edge, not a large one. The likely mispricing is that the market is treating a roughly 6-7% cushion as almost sufficient for near-certainty even though settlement depends on one exact Binance minute close.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

This is not a material departure from the swarm-implied center of about 0.90. If anything I stayed close to it because the raw lanes were unusually aligned and the synthesis-stage verification confirmed the core factual setup rather than overturning it.

## Edge verification status

Independent verification quality is medium. I independently checked the Polymarket rule text and current Binance ETHUSDT data during synthesis. …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market is broadly right that Yes is the base case because Binance ETH/USDT is comfortably above 2200, but 94.5% looks slightly too high for a venue-specific one-minute crypto close that is still nearly a day away.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of 94.5% Yes.
Check the governing source of truth and exact settlement mechanics on Polymarket rules.
Verify current Binance ETH/USDT level and short-horizon context directly on Binance data.
Conclude that Yes is the base case, but trim confidence modestly for tail-risk and venue-specific settlement mechanics.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ETHUSDT checks were around 2352.5-2352.6, well above 2200.
Binance 24h low in the check was 2308.5, still above the threshold.
strongest_disconfirmers: Crypto can still move more than 6% in less than a day.
Settlement is venue-specific and minute-specific, so a Binance-only wick or operational anomaly could matter.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance website chart presentation and public API would ever diverge in a way that matters for settlement.
How much tail-risk should be charged over the remaining sub-24h window.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if Binance ETH/USDT moved materially closer to 2200, if Binance underperformed other spot references, or if an exchange-specific operational/data issue appeared near settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Very high: the market resolves on the Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute candle close, and the remaining window is short enough that current spot matters but long enough for crypto tail-risk to remain live.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality direct venue data plus clear contract rules, with only medium independence because most important evidence points back to the same underlying Binance market.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is still the right side because ETH/USDT is materially above 2200 close to resolution, but the market slightly overstates certainty for a one-minute Binance-close contract.
main_logical_chain: Contract asks about one specific Binance ETH/USDT minute close above 2200 at noon ET on Apr 17.
Current ETH/USDT context is materially above 2200, so the outside-view prior favors Yes.
Because crypto is volatile and the market settles on one minute rather than a broad average, near-certainty is too aggressive.
Therefore Yes remains favored, but at about 88% rather than the market's 94.5%.
strongest_supports: ETH/USDT contextual spot was around the mid-2300s during research, leaving a roughly 6-7% cushion above 2200.
Less than a day remained to resolution, favoring the currently above-strike side absent a sharp selloff.
strongest_disconfirmers: Settlement depends on a single Binance one-minute close at noon ET, so a brief downside move is enough for No.
Recent context included at least one nearby daily close below 2200, showing sub-2200 prints are still plausible.
unresolved_ambiguities: Direct live extraction from Binance was limited, leaving small residual ET-to-candle mapping uncertainty.
No fresh Apr 17 morning check yet; current view depends on Apr 16 spot context persisting.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A fresh check showing ETH/USDT near or below 2200 on Apr 17 morning, or Binance-specific operational problems near settlement, would move the estimate down materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: This is a date- and timezone-sensitive single-minute market resolving at 12:00 PM ET on 2026-04-17, so proximity to resolution and intraday volatility matter heavily.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High confidence in contract mechanics from Polymarket rules, moderate confidence in contextual price level from independent price pages/search snippets, with medium evidence independence overall.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: ETH is likely to stay above 2200 at the Binance noon ET resolving minute, but the single-minute close mechanic keeps residual downside timing risk nontrivial.
main_logical_chain: Contract resolves on one exact Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 17.
Current Binance spot is materially above 2200 and recent sampled range stayed above the threshold.
No clearly identified scheduled catalyst looks large enough on its own to erase that cushion before resolution.
Residual risk remains because a sharp selloff into the exact resolving minute could still flip the market to No.
strongest_supports: Binance ETH/USDT last price sampled at 2353.84, well above 2200.
Binance 24h low sampled at 2308.50, still above 2200.
strongest_disconfirmers: Single-minute resolution creates path-sensitive downside risk even if ETH trades above 2200 most of the time.
Crypto can move more than 6% intraday on shock headlines or liquidation.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether any unscheduled macro or crypto headline emerges before the resolving window.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A decisive break toward/below 2300, a new bearish catalyst, or Binance-specific instability near resolution would cut the Yes probability materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: This case is mainly about whether the current >2200 cushion survives into the exact noon ET resolving minute, not about ETH direction in a broad daily sense.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Source-of-truth ambiguity is low because the contract is explicit, but evidence independence is only medium because the thesis leans heavily on Binance and contract mechanics.
